# Phase 20 — Track A INSTALL.md Walk-through (Author-walked, with deviation per D-02)

**Date:** [walker fills]
**Walker:** cnoellert — project orchestrator / author of the planning artifacts; **non-author of the install procedure itself** (the doc text was generated by Claude from codebase inference; this is the first end-to-end physical walk). See "Deviations" section, item 0, for the formal D-02 deviation record.
**Operator workstation:** flame-01 (preferred — cleaner) OR portofino (dev box) — walker records which one was actually used
**LLM service host (separate; reached over the network):** assist-01 @ `192.168.86.15` — runs Ollama + `qwen2.5-coder:32b`. The walker does NOT touch this host during the walk; it must be reachable from the operator workstation.
**Branch / HEAD commit:** e331821 (or descendant — record the actual SHA you walked at)
**INSTALL.md commit:** [same as branch HEAD unless explicitly different]
**FORGE_LOCAL_LLM_URL the operator sets in Step 5:** `http://192.168.86.15:11434/v1`

**Discipline contract for this walk** (because the walker has author context that a stranger wouldn't):
1. Copy-paste every command verbatim from `docs/INSTALL.md`. Do NOT mentally substitute corrections or fill-ins.
2. Type out the observed output verbatim into the corresponding Step section — even if it matches the doc's prediction. The act of transcribing forces an honest read.
3. Do NOT silently route around problems. If a command fails or the doc is ambiguous, STOP, log the gap with a disposition, and let the author patch INSTALL.md inline before re-walking that step. Silent routing-around is what makes author walks lie; explicit gap-logging is what keeps this honest.
4. Flag every spot you had to draw on knowledge not in the doc, even if it didn't break the walk. Log as a deviation. Examples: knowing assist-01's IP from outside the doc; knowing which conda env to use; knowing that Postgres needed initdb. These are exactly the comprehension gaps a non-author would surface.

## Pre-walk setup (author-prepared on each host, ✓ when complete)

**Operator workstation** (flame-01 or portofino — the host the non-author will walk on):

- [ ] Pre-existing `forge` conda env removed (`conda env remove -n forge` if it existed)
- [ ] Postgres daemon running locally on `:5432` (or operator will install it during INSTALL.md Step 3 — record which path applies)
- [ ] Any prior Flame hook removed (`rm -f /opt/Autodesk/shared/python/forge_bridge/scripts/forge_bridge.py` so the install step does real work)
- [ ] Flame is installed and runnable (Track A only)
- [ ] Phase 20 branch checked out at HEAD with Plans 20-01 through 20-04 + 20-07 landed (HEAD `854704a` or descendant)
- [ ] `docs/INSTALL.md` open in a separate window for the non-author to follow
- [ ] Network reachability confirmed from this host: `curl -s http://192.168.86.15:11434/api/version` returns JSON (proves assist-01 is reachable)

**LLM service host** (assist-01 @ 192.168.86.15 — the non-author does NOT touch this; the author pre-verifies before handoff):

- [ ] Ollama daemon running on `:11434`, listening on the network interface (not just localhost) so the operator workstation can reach it
- [ ] `qwen2.5-coder:32b` pulled (`ollama list | grep qwen2.5-coder` on assist-01)
- [ ] Firewall on assist-01 permits inbound `:11434` from the operator workstation's subnet

## Gap log

Each gap surfaced during the walk gets a disposition per CONTEXT.md D-04 / D-05 / D-06:
- **doc-only** → patch INSTALL.md inline; re-walk the affected step. Do NOT close the UAT until the patched doc walks clean.
- **code-fix (≤1 plan)** → spin a Phase 20 follow-up plan (20-08, 20-09, ... — note that 20-06 is Track B and 20-07 was the multi-host topology fix). Block the UAT until the code-fix plan lands.
- **code-fix (>1 plan)** → spin a 20.1 decimal phase per D-05. UAT outcome is FAIL until the decimal phase closes.
- **v1.6+ deferred** → log here, plant a seed in `.planning/seeds/`. Do not block the UAT.

| # | Step | Gap description | Disposition | Plan / seed that addressed it |
|---|------|----------------|-------------|-------------------------------|
| [non-author + author fill collaboratively as gaps surface] | | | | |

## Deviations

Anything the walker did that wasn't strictly verbatim from INSTALL.md, with justification:

0. **D-02 deviation — author-walked rather than non-author-walked (pre-recorded; required acknowledgment).** The walker (cnoellert) is the author of the Phase 20 planning artifacts but a non-author of the install procedure itself — the doc text was generated by Claude from codebase inference and this is the first end-to-end physical walk. A separate, fully-non-author UAT was not feasible (no secondary operator available). Mitigation: walker honored the discipline contract above (verbatim copy-paste, verbatim observation transcription, no silent routing-around, explicit logging of any author-context dependence). Outcome should be recorded as `PASS-with-deviations` at best, never `PASS`. A future fresh non-author UAT is captured as a seed in `.planning/seeds/SEED-PHASE-20-NON-AUTHOR-UAT.md`.

1. [walker + author fill collaboratively as deviations surface]
